examples.excerpt: Since 2000, China has experienced the highest patent growth rates of any of the top 15 inventor locations. Between 2000 and 2019, Chinese patent families expanded by compound rate of 38% per year. However, China’s patent growth has slowed in recent years, leading to an annual growth rate of around 14% between 2019 and 2024 (Figure 3.6). Consequently, since 2019, India, Sweden, Canada and France have surpassed China’s patent growth rates, albeit from a much lower starting point.

Sweden has achieved the highest patent growth rate in the area of green digitalization for heavy‑duty road transport, at a compound rate of 27% per annum (Figure 6.4). India, Israel, the Netherlands and China have also demonstrated notable double‑digit growth.

Patent growth and specialization – In the field of vehicle efficiency for heavy‑duty road transport, India has emerged as the fastest‑growing innovation location, showing a surge in patent publications from 66 in 2019 to 300 in 2024. This corresponds to a robust 35% CAGR. Sweden was the only other major research location to exhibit strong momentum, recording an average annual increase in published patent families of 11%.
